analyst_observations: The pass-rate delta comes entirely from eval-2 (code review): baseline missed try?-swallowing and concurrency-safety, the two 'review depth' assertions. Evals 0-1 pass for both configs — the model is already strong on mechanical migration and greenfield actors, so those assertions don't discriminate; consider tightening them in iteration 2.

Qualitative gaps the assertions under-count: with-skill implemented in-flight request deduplication (baseline only discussed it in prose); with-skill injected a test Clock giving 10/10 deterministic tests with zero real sleeps, while baseline tests sleep on a real ContinuousClock (flake risk); with-skill review found 15 issues vs ~7 baseline.

Cost of the skill: roughly +97s and +21.5k tokens per task (reference reading + more compile verification). Reasonable for correctness-critical work; heavy for trivial snippets — SKILL.md's 'when NOT to over-apply' section is doing necessary work.
High time stddev in with-skill config is driven by the RateKit package eval (376s) which built and ran tests repeatedly.
